metric_value: 'Until Prime's Medusa and other CAD products establish our market presence, we are under constant comparison to the stand-alone design vendors like Applicon and Computervision. If Prime could offer a $50,000 (hardware cost) system for a single workstation, with full Prime functionality and expandability, our competitive position would be strengthened.'

metric_name: Apple II vs Prime 250 reference frame
metric_value: 'An Apple II computer can support 10MB of disk, a single user process, and 48K of memory. A Prime 250 can support 2000MB of disk, 64 processes, and a megabyte of memory. There is only a tenfold difference in price.'
notes: Direct Apple II / Prime 250 comparison defending host-timesharing model — anchored in 1981; relevance shifted dramatically by 1984

metric_value: 'Prime's excellent communications capabilities can be enhanced so that Prime provides a computer which translates one word processor's communications to what is understandable by another vendor's word processor, facilitating the movement of documents in the many organizations with mixed vendor's equipment. We recommend that Prime pursue this niche.'
notes: Anticipates document-translation gateway / format-conversion services
